insight: Argentina's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows that they were able to gain momentum in half of the breaks, with a slight overall decline in momentum at a rate of -0.150 per minute. However, when they were pressing before a break, they tended to lose momentum at a higher rate of -0.400 per minute, and were unable to regain it after the break. In contrast, when Argentina was not pressing before a break, they were able to slightly increase their momentum at a rate of +0.100 per minute, suggesting that they may benefit from a more conservative approach leading up to a hydration break. This indicates that Argentina's strategy going into a break can significantly impact their performance coming out of it, and they may need to adjust their approach to maximize their chances of success.
